name: snf-automation-plugins
description: Speed & Function Automation department's Claude Code plugins — internal tooling for the MN Service (Meeting Notes) pipeline and daily call digests.

plugins.name: mn-team-links
plugins.description: Resolve an MN Service team to its Google Drive transcript/notes folder link, read live from the n8n MN Service config via the connected n8n MCP. Read-only; prints a Team -> Drive-folder table. Requires the n8n MCP connected.

plugins.name: mn-team-onboard
plugins.description: Onboard a new team to the MN Service pipeline from a team tag + parent Drive folder link: creates Drive subfolders via a token-protected n8n webhook and adds one teamsRaw routing entry (Stage then Prod), self-validated with rollback. Writer skill; requires the n8n MCP + Google Drive connector + webhook token.

plugins.name: daily-call-tasks
plugins.description: One skill, one command for the calls you attended. A scheduled run prints a read-only, cited TABLE of your action items for yesterday (one table per meeting — heading name·date·participants; columns № | task | priority | status | deadline | assignee | description; continuous numbering across all tables). A manual run asks the period (+ optional participants/team), renders the same tables, and on your confirmation pushes the chosen tasks to ClickUp's automation space with status/priority/deadline/assignee (team-assign allowed). Sonnet sub-agents per call; never invents an item. Requires Google Calendar + Drive (+ ClickUp MCP for the push).

plugins.name: morning-brief
plugins.description: Interactive Geekbot-style standup prep — one command, no flags. Runs the daily-call-tasks call extraction (Sonnet) as a source, then walks you through a status-management step that lists ALL your tasks by status and applies the ClickUp status changes you command, then what-was-done, on-your-plate (you pick what to report), blockers with their reason, your open questions, and a Mood picked from your standup's real Geekbot options — and posts the standup to Geekbot (→ Slack) with correct mentions and each task as a clickable ClickUp link. Setup is transparent on first run. Read-only except the status changes you command + the confirmed Geekbot post. Requires Google Calendar + Drive + ClickUp; optional Geekbot key / Gmail / team.md.

plugins.name: ai-digest
plugins.description: Drafts the automation department's weekly cross-department digest (top-3 Closed-this-week / In-progress / Priorities) from the team's own meeting-notes (primary narrative) + ClickUp (the dated 'what closed' signal). Notes-led, every line cited, print-only — a human edits the draft and the lead publishes; never auto-posts. Read-only against Drive + ClickUp; makes no 'verified' claims (the citation is the honesty). Optional Geekbot enrich-only 3rd source (OFF unless a key is configured). Requires the ClickUp MCP + a Google Drive connector.
